Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-09-090
Dear Ms. Maiques:
This letter is inresponse to your request on behalf of the City of whittier, city
council for advice regarding the gift provisions of the political Reform Act lttre ;esrj'r
Because your question seeks geneial guidance and does not reference any specific
proceedings, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
what are the website posting and retention requirements for the Tickets provided
by Agency Report (Form SOi)t
CONCLUSION
Regulation 18944.r requires an agency to post the Form g02 on the agency,s
website within 30 days of the distribution of t-he tickets. This form should be posted on
the agency's website for four years. The original Form g02 shall be retained by the
filing officer for a period ofnot Iess than seven years.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Secrions g1000 through gl0l4. AII
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Secrions l8l l0 throug h lgggl of ritle 2 of the california
Code of Regulations' All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division'6 of the California code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicited.
r Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal wrirten advice. (Section g3 I I 4; h,egulation I g329(cX3). )

on March 24,2009 Whittier's city council adopted a Gift Ticket Distribution
Policy as required under Regulation 18944.1. The City has a website and you will be
responsible for posting and maintaining the forms.
F'ACTS
ANALYSIS
scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Tobola
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Regulation I8944.l
- Recently, the Commission adopted amendments to Regul ationtg944.l. This
regulation provides an optional procedure for a public agency to use as a method for
distributing tickets or passes to events. If the provisions"of the regulation are followed,
certain tickets or passes will not be considered gifts to the users bEcause adequate
consideration is provided. You seek clarificati; only on the issue "r t""uriti posting and
the retention period for the Form g02.
Website Posting
Regulation 18944.1(d) states the Form 802 shall be posted in a prominent fashion
on the agency's website within 30 days after ticket distribution. The norm g0- requires
the agency head or his/trer designee to verify the report *itr, u signature. When the
posted information is a copy of the paper report, it must be mainiained on the agency,s
website for four years. (Section Sf OOOfO.l 
-
Retention Of Original Statement
Regulation l^8?!:l does not specifically state a time period for retention of the
Form 802' Section 81009(e) requires triginal ,Lpo.tr, orr,o,it * .u-puig, oi statement
of economic interest forms, to be retain"J-fo, ."u", y"*r. th"r"rore, tlJ"rig*a Form
802 shall be retained by the filing officer for a period of not less than r"r"., fE-..
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me
at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
MCTjgl
